unit.note: server vitest 179 files / 2092 passed + 1 skipped (+7 vs main: the new terminal-spawn-failure.test.ts). Server-only diff; client suite untouched (2025 passed on main). tsc --noEmit clean (server + client); oxlint clean on all touched files.

integration.note: No integration surface: a robustness fix to the existing pty spawn boundary (typed error + caller handling). No new route/store/contract/cross-component machinery.

e2e.note: F0.5 web: 73-embedded-terminal.spec.ts on an isolated single-process stack (:3947, temp USERPROFILE, 127.0.0.1), exit 0. Proves the try/catch wrappers on the WS-upgrade + prewarm spawn seams do NOT regress the happy path — test #3 (WS upgrade emits ready) + #4/#5 (paste-image via prewarm spawn) all pass. The vanished-cwd ERROR path is covered deterministically by the server unit tests (a real cwd race is not reliably reproducible in one browser E2E).

design_fidelity.note: No UI change — server-only robustness fix; the only client-visible effect is that a WS upgrade against a vanished cwd now closes cleanly instead of an opaque failure.

smoke.note: Isolated single-process stack (built client + SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR + tsx on :3947, temp USERPROFILE, 127.0.0.1): GET /api/external/tasks 200.

test_completeness.enumeration_basis: Every behavior the diff introduces or changes, enumerated from the 3 spec ACs (AC-1 typed error + no-leak, AC-2 WS clean rejection, AC-3 prewarm status split) plus the sub-behaviors those ACs decompose into (code parsing, isLikelyCwdError predicate, whitelist-vs-cwd-vs-other status mapping) and the happy-path non-regression. 8 behaviors; all tested; 0 untested-testable. The root-cause claim (267 = ERROR_DIRECTORY, NOT resource exhaustion) was proven empirically by 3 node-pty probes before the fix — diagnostic, not a committed test.

behaviors.behavior: spawn() wraps a node-pty CreateProcess throw in PtySpawnFailedError and leaves NO pty entry registered (no leak)
PtySpawnFailedError.code parses the native code (267) from the message; null when absent
isLikelyCwdError is true for 267/ENOENT/ENOTDIR, false for EMFILE/EPERM
WS upgrade converts a spawn failure into a deterministic task_cwd_unusable rejection instead of an uncaught node-pty throw
behaviors.evidence: terminal-spawn-failure.test.ts AC-2 (fake upgradeWebSocket captures the rejection reason)

behaviors.behavior: Prewarm POST /spawn returns 409 task_cwd_unusable on a vanished-cwd (267) spawn failure
Prewarm keeps 400 pty_spawn_rejected for a whitelist rejection (unchanged)
Prewarm returns 500 pty_spawn_failed for a NON-cwd spawn failure (EMFILE), not mislabelled as task_cwd_unusable
The terminal WS-upgrade + spawn + ready happy path (and prewarm-backed paste-image) is unregressed by the try/catch wrappers
